description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust frame. The solar panels are dark, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, and are supported by a metallic structure with visible struts and braces. The base of the unit contains electrical components, possibly including a control box or inverter, with some red and gray elements suggesting power electronics or safety features. The frame appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing stability and durability. The overall design is modular and low-profile, suitable for ground installation in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or small utility areas. The unit is engineered for efficient solar energy capture and integration into local power grids or off-grid systems.
description: A compact, ground-mounted solar panel array with a sturdy metal frame and integrated electrical components at the base. The unit features dark photovoltaic panels and a modular, urban-friendly design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the solar panel array and the electrical component housing at the base, with visible red and gray details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the supporting metal struts, with the base and electrical components partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and the structural frame, with less detail on the electrical components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel tilt and the full length of the supporting frame, with the base and red elements visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and support struts
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 3-4 solar panels arranged linearly
